content.problemStatement:
Given an array of NNN numbers, write a program to check if a given number xxx exists in the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two space separated numbers NNN and xxx - the length of array and the element to be searched.
The second line of input contains NNN space separated numbers - the elements of the array.


Output Format

Output on a single line, `YES` if xxx is present in the array, else `NO`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line of a test case contains two space-separated integers N and x – the length of the array and the value to search for. The second line contains N space-separated integers, the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if x appears in the array, otherwise "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array contains the value 7, so the answer is YES.
The value 10 does not appear in the array, therefore the answer is NO.
The single element equals the searched value, so the output is YES.

solution.approach: The task is to check the presence of a value in an unsorted array. The simplest and optimal method is a single linear scan of the N elements. While reading the numbers we compare each with x; if any matches we set a flag. After processing the whole array we output YES if the flag is true, otherwise NO. This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int n,x; scanf("%d %d",&n,&x); int found=0; for(int i=0;i<n;++i){int v; scanf("%d",&v); if(v==x) found=1;} printf(found?"YES\n":"NO\n");} return 0; }
code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int T=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int tc=0;tc<T;tc++){st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int n=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int x=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());boolean found=false;for(int i=0;i<n;i++){int v=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());if(v==x) found=true;}sb.append(found?"YES":"NO");if(tc<T-1) sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
